e-crete.title: E-Crete
e-crete.implementation: Replace Ordinary Portland Cement (OPC) with E-Crete geopolymer cement. Reduces CO2 by 80% compared to OPC through industrial by-product utilization. Requires structural verification for specific applications.
e-crete.limitations: Limited regional availability; requires supply chain development; structural performance may vary by application type.

oxacrete.title: Oxacrete
oxacrete.implementation: Utilize Oxacrete technology which achieves 90% CO2 reduction through mineralization processes. Concrete hardens while sequestering carbon dioxide. Verify local contractor availability and curing time compatibility.
oxacrete.limitations: Emerging technology with limited field data; higher material cost; curing time may extend project schedules; contractor experience varies.

issb.title: Interlocking Stabilized Soil Blocks (ISSB)
issb.implementation: Replace fired clay bricks with interlocking stabilized soil blocks (ISSB). Eliminates kiln energy (85% reduction vs conventional bricks). Requires local soil assessment and trained mason workforce.
issb.limitations: Availability depends on local manufacturing capacity; architectural aesthetics differ from fired bricks; durability in high-moisture environments requires validation.

aect-ceb.title: AECT CEB Machine
aect-ceb.alternative: AECT Impact 2001A - Compressed Earth Block machine
aect-ceb.implementation: Deploy AECT CEB (Compressed Earth Block) machine for on-site block production. Reduces embodied carbon 80% vs kiln-fired bricks. Requires local soil surveys and equipment operator training.
aect-ceb.sourceTitle: AECT CEB Machine - Engineering for Change
aect-ceb.limitations: Capital investment for machinery (~$50-100K); soil quality-dependent; limited to certain climates; performance certification required for structural applications.

g2e-biomass.title: G2E Biomass Gasification
g2e-biomass.implementation: Install G2E biomass gasification system for thermal energy generation. Converts agricultural/industrial waste to syngas for heating/power. Produces carbon-negative biochar byproduct. Requires feedstock supply chain.
g2e-biomass.sourceTitle: G2E Biomass Gasification - Engineering for Change
g2e-biomass.limitations: High capital cost ($200K+); requires consistent biomass feedstock; emissions accounting complex due to biogenic/biochar sequestration; air quality permits needed.

cl-06-solar-thermal.title: CL-06 Solar Thermal
cl-06-solar-thermal.implementation: Deploy CL-06 solar thermal trough system for industrial process heat (>150°C). Achieves 76.9% efficiency; suitable for food processing, mineral processing, textile drying. Requires solar resource assessment and hybrid backup design.
cl-06-solar-thermal.sourceTitle: CL-06 Solar Thermal - Engineering for Change
cl-06-solar-thermal.limitations: Geographic limitation to high insolation regions; seasonal variability; requires thermal storage or gas backup; high upfront CAPEX ($150-300K).

lean-briqs.title: Lean Briqs
lean-briqs.implementation: Replace coal/diesel fuels with Lean Briqs compressed biomass briquettes. Each tonne of briquettes saves ~1 tonne CO2 vs coal. Direct substitution in existing boilers/furnaces after minimal modifications.
lean-briqs.limitations: Supply chain maturity varies by region; feedstock sustainability practices must be verified; ash handling/disposal procedures differ from coal; higher storage volume needed.

biochar-maroc.title: BioChar Maroc
biochar-maroc.implementation: Incorporate BioChar Maroc into agricultural soils for carbon sequestration and yield improvement. Converts agricultural waste to stable carbon. Suitable for regenerative agriculture and soil remediation projects.
biochar-maroc.limitations: Long-term sequestration verification required; soil type dependent; benefits vary by climate; requires agronomic expertise; permanence timescale (100+ years) requires validation.

reppie-waste-energy.title: Reppie Waste-to-Energy
reppie-waste-energy.implementation: Develop waste-to-energy facility with grid export capability (similar to Reppie model in Ethiopia). Anaerobic digestion + biofuel generation. Reduces landfill methane while generating renewable energy.
reppie-waste-energy.limitations: Requires large capital investment ($5M+); regulatory framework and grid interconnection agreements needed; long development timeline (2-3 years); feedstock quality variability.

styro-densifiers.title: Styro-plastic Densifiers
styro-densifiers.implementation: Install styrofoam/plastic densification equipment to compress waste by 50:1 ratio. Enables economical recycling transport and reprocessing. Integrates with circular economy supply chains.
styro-densifiers.limitations: Equipment cost ($30-80K); operational training required; market for densified material must be established; disposal of processing residues needed.
